reasoning: Q3 is 5-8x slower than DuckDB (615ms vs 78-113ms) with clear high-impact optimizations available. Previous iteration regressed from 386ms baseline, but correctness is perfect. Zone map skipping alone offers 40-50% speedup by avoiding unnecessary I/O. With 1 iteration left, aggressively pursue the top 3 low-risk, complementary optimizations.
focus_areas: Implement zone map block skipping for o_orderdate and l_shipdate predicates to skip ~50% of blocks
Add bloom filter on qualified_orders to reduce hash table probe overhead by 80-90%
Fix madvise hints: use MADV_RANDOM for join keys, MADV_WILLNEED for small scan columns
strategy_notes: This is our final iteration. Priority: (1) Zone map skipping is THE critical optimization - zone maps are loaded but never consulted, causing 100% block scans despite 50% selectivity predicates. Implementation must check zone boundaries before processing each morsel. (2) Bloom filter reduces cache thrashing from 3M-entry hash table probes. (3) Adaptive madvise fixes OS-level I/O inefficiency for random join key access. These three optimizations are complementary (I/O reduction, cache optimization, OS hint tuning) and all low-risk. Target: 300-350ms (2-3x gap from DuckDB is acceptable if correctness maintained).
notes: Avoid the mistakes from iteration 1 that caused regression. Zone map implementation requires careful morsel-to-zone-index mapping. Bloom filter sizing: 469KB fits in L2 cache. Do not over-optimize - focus on these 3 proven techniques.
